channel.title: General on A Blog
channel.description: Recent content in General on A Blog

item.title: Arrow Turns 10
item.description: I gave a talk to open the 2025 Arrow Summit in Paris, looking back at the history of the Apache Arrow project, some factors that have contributed to its success, and what the past can tell us about how to ensure a bright future. The talk wasn’t recorded, and while I’ve published the slides, they don’t alone capture what the talk was like, so I thought I would publish a version of it in blog form here.

item.title: Talk Slides Now Available
item.description: In putting up the slides for the talk I gave at last week’s Bay Area R meetup, I figured it would be good to dig up the slides from previous talks and post them here too. (In fact, I linked to one of them in the slides from last week.) So, there’s now a “Talks” section of the site.

item.title: Building a Blogdown Site with Travis-CI
item.description:
Over on the Crunch.io dev blog, I wrote about how we used Travis-CI to automate the building of our various static sites. I thought I’d do the same for my personal blog, though with one twist: using R and the blogdown package on top of Hugo.

This blogdown GitHub issue pointed to Yihui’s guide to building bookdown projects on Travis, as well as a project that had adapted that flow for blogdown. Both were helpful, and with my prior experience, I was able to build on those examples to come up with a simple workflow. It was shockingly straightforward.
